summary: vue-demi-fix typosquats vue-demi and exfiltrates installer host identifiers via preinstall to hardcoded bare IP
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle hook that runs at npm install time and issues curl requests to http://109.71.252.153:8080/ over plain HTTP, sending the installer's Windows username (%USERNAME%), current working directory (%CD%), and hostname (%COMPUTERNAME%) as query string parameters. The package name 'vue-demi-fix' impersonates the widely-used 'vue-demi' package, and the tarball contains only a stub module plus this preinstall hook — there is no legitimate functionality. README self-describes as a proof-of-concept. Installer harm fires automatically on `npm install` with no user opt-in: every install transmits host-identifying reconnaissance data to an attacker-controlled bare-IP endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
